mission.title: cilium: Scale-down event of EKS kube-apiserver causes network outage
mission.description: Scale-down event of EKS kube-apiserver causes network outage. This issue affects 22+ users.

steps.title: Identify cilium troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### What happened?

AWS EKS scales its kube-apiserver(s) out and in.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Scale-down event of EKS kube-apiserver causes network outage
steps.description:
While a 404 Not Found or a 409 Conflict can be considered successful interactions with the k8s API, a blanket accept for all 4xx codes is problematic. Since LastSuccessInteraction is exclusively used as an optimisation, we should err on the cautious side: accept the potential increase in heartbeats
```yaml
cilium-operator-78d4dc7dbb-lv8q2 cilium-operator error retrieving resource lock kube-system/cilium-operator-resource-lock: Unauthorized
cilium-operator-78d4dc7dbb-lv8q2 cilium-operator level=error msg="error retrieving resource lock kube-system/cilium-operator-resource-lock: Unauthorized" subsys=klo
```

steps.title: Confirm Scale-down event of EKS kube-apiserver causes… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: While a 404 Not Found or a 409 Conflict can be considered successful interactions with the k8s API, a blanket accept for all 4xx codes is problematic. Since LastSuccessInteraction is exclusively used as an optimisation, we should err on the cautious side: accept the potential increase in heartbeats to avoid missing being unable to effecticely communicate with the k8s API.
codeSnippets:
cilium-operator-78d4dc7dbb-lv8q2 cilium-operator error retrieving resource lock kube-system/cilium-operator-resource-lock: Unauthorized
cilium-operator-78d4dc7dbb-lv8q2 cilium-operator level=error msg="error retrieving resource lock kube-system/cilium-operator-resource-lock: Unauthorized" subsys=klo

affinity:
  nodeAffinity:
    requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
      nodeSelectorTerms:
      - matchExpressions:
        - key: kubernetes.io/os
          operator: In
          values:
          - linux
        - key: kubernetes.io/arch
          operator: In
          values:
          - amd64
        - key: eks.amazonaws.com/compute-type
          operator: NotIn
          values:
          - fargate
bpf:
  hostRouting: false
  masquerade: true
  tproxy: true
cni:
  chainingMode: none
  configMap: cni-configuration
  customConf: true
devices: eth0
enableCiliumEndpointSlice: true
endpointHealthChecking:
  enabled: false
eni:
  awsEnablePrefixDelegation: true
  ec2APIEndpoint: ec2.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com
  enabled: true
  instanceTagsFilter: aws:eks:cluster-name=ci-eks-4qr

❯ cilium version
cilium-cli: 0.12.1 compiled with go1.18.5 on linux/amd64
cilium image (default): v1.12.0
cilium image (stable): v1.12.0
cilium image (running): v1.12.0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
